member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.AvalonTextEditorBindingBehavior
member.summary:
AvalonEdit TextEditor binding behavior that allows for binding of the text property, the selected text property, the selection property, and the cursor position property. Note, that this is useful as long as the TextEditor does not have large quantities of text. AvalonEdit is built for large amounts of text (storing them in Ropes) and this turns those in real time into strings which can be excessively resource consuming.
<avalonedit:TextEditor> <i:Interaction.Behaviors> <behaviors:AvalonTextEditorBindingBehavior Text="{Binding TextProperty}" SelectedText="{Binding SelectedTextProperty}" Selection="{Binding SelectionProperty}" CursorPosition="{Binding CursorPositionProperty}" /> </i:Interaction.Behaviors> </avalonedit:TextEditor>

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.AvalonEditCopyBehavior
member.summary: A behavior that enables a to copy text from a specified to the clipboard.

remarks.code: <TextBox x:Name="MyTextBox" Width="200" Height="25" /> <Button Content="Copy"> <i:Interaction.Behaviors> <behaviors1:AvalonEditCopyBehavior TargetTextEditor="{Binding ElementName=XamlEditor}" /> </i:Interaction.Behaviors> </Button>

member.summary: Gets or sets the target instance associated with this property.

member.summary: When the behavior is attached to the associated object (a ),

member.summary: When the behavior is detached to the associated object (a ),

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.AvalonEditCopyBehavior.OnButtonClick(System.Object,System.Windows.RoutedEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles the button click event to copy text from the target text box to the clipboard.
member.remarks: If the is null, the method exits without performing any action. The method prioritizes copying the selected text from the target text box. If no text is selected, it copies the entire text content of the text box. If the text to copy is empty or null, no action is taken. Any exceptions that occur while setting the clipboard text are logged using .
member.param: The source of the event, typically the button that was clicked.
The event data associated with the button click.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.AvalonEditVtTerminalBehavior
member.summary:
A behavior that applies a retro VT/CRT terminal visual skin to an AvalonEdit . When active the editor receives a phosphor-green color scheme, a solid block caret, and visual overlay effects (CRT scan lines, screen vignette, phosphor glow, and subtle noise). Toggle the skin at runtime via the dependency property.
<avalonedit:TextEditor> <i:Interaction.Behaviors> <behaviors:AvalonEditVtTerminalBehavior IsActive="{Binding IsTerminalMode}" /> </i:Interaction.Behaviors> </avalonedit:TextEditor>

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.AvalonEditVtTerminalBehavior.IsActive
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the VT terminal skin is active.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.AvalonEditVtTerminalBehavior.ForegroundColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the phosphor foreground color. Defaults to classic green (#33FF33).

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.AvalonEditVtTerminalBehavior.BackgroundColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the terminal background color. Defaults to near-black (#050505).

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.VtTerminalBlockCaretRenderer
member.summary: Renders a solid block cursor at the caret position, replacing the default thin line cursor.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.VtTerminalAdorner
member.summary: An adorner that renders CRT-style visual effects over the adorned element: scan lines, screen vignette, phosphor glow, and subtle noise texture.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.BlockCaretBehavior
member.summary: A behavior that replaces the standard I-beam caret in a with a full-character-width block caret, similar to terminal emulators and classic text editors.

remarks.para: The behavior hides the native TextBox caret by setting to transparent and adds an that draws a solid rectangle at the current caret position. Optionally the character under the caret is re-drawn on top of the block in the contrasting color (controlled by ) so the text remains legible. The block is only visible while the TextBox has keyboard focus and automatically disappears when a range of text is selected. The adorner is cleaned up when the behavior is detached or the TextBox is unloaded.

The block width is measured per-character using so it tracks proportional fonts correctly. The typeface is cached and only re-created when the font properties change. Scroll position is tracked via the internal so the block stays in sync when the user scrolls a multi-line TextBox with the mouse wheel.

member.example:
Minimal usage — block color defaults to the TextBox Foreground:
<TextBox FontFamily="Consolas" FontSize="14"> <i:Interaction.Behaviors> <behaviors:BlockCaretBehavior /> </i:Interaction.Behaviors> </TextBox>

Terminal-style green block with white character overlay:
<TextBox FontFamily="Consolas" FontSize="14" Background="Black" Foreground="LimeGreen"> <i:Interaction.Behaviors> <behaviors:BlockCaretBehavior BlockBrush="LimeGreen" CharacterBrush="Black" /> </i:Interaction.Behaviors> </TextBox>

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.BlockCaretBehavior.BlockBrushProperty
member.summary: The brush used to fill the block caret rectangle. When (the default) the TextBox's is used.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.BlockCaretBehavior.BlockBrush
member.summary: The brush used to fill the block caret rectangle. When (the default) the TextBox's is used.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.BlockCaretBehavior.CharacterBrushProperty
member.summary: The brush used to draw the character that sits under the caret (the "inverted" foreground). When (the default) the TextBox's is used.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.BlockCaretBehavior.CharacterBrush
member.summary: The brush used to draw the character that sits under the caret (the "inverted" foreground). When (the default) the TextBox's is used.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.BlockCaretBehavior.ShowCharacterUnderCaretProperty
member.summary: When (the default) the character under the caret is re-rendered on top of the block in so the text remains legible. Set to for a solid opaque block that hides the character.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.BlockCaretBehavior.ShowCharacterUnderCaret
member.summary: When (the default) the character under the caret is re-rendered on top of the block in so the text remains legible. Set to for a solid opaque block that hides the character.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.DataGridFilterBehavior
member.summary: Behavior that will allow a to filter a .
member.remarks:
This behavior allows a to filter the items in a . To use this behavior, attach it to a and bind the property to the you want to filter.
Example usage in XAML:
<Window x:Class="YourNamespace.MainWindow" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation" xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml" xmlns:behaviors="clr-namespace:ApexGate.UI.Behaviors" Title="DataGrid Filter Example" Height="350" Width="525"> <Grid> <Grid.RowDefinitions> <RowDefinition Height="Auto" /> <RowDefinition Height="*" /> </Grid.RowDefinitions>
<!-- TextBox for filtering --> <TextBox Width="200" Margin="10" VerticalAlignment="Top"> <i:Interaction.Behaviors> <behaviors:DataGridFilterBehavior TargetDataGrid = "{Binding ElementName=MyDataGrid}" /> </ i:Interaction.Behaviors> </TextBox>
<!-- DataGrid to be filtered --> <DataGrid x:Name="MyDataGrid" Grid.Row="1" Margin="10" AutoGenerateColumns="True" /> </Grid> </Window>

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.DataGridFilterBehavior.TargetDataGridProperty
member.summary: to filter.

member.summary: to filter.

member.summary: When the is attached.

member.summary: When the is detached.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.FocusBehavior
member.summary:
Sets the focus to the control when it is loaded. If this is used on multiple controls the last control it's used on will get the focus.
<Window xmlns:b="clr-namespace:Sandbox.Behaviors" xmlns:i="http://schemas.microsoft.com/xaml/behaviors"> <TextBox> <i:Interaction.Behaviors> <b:FocusBehavior /> </i:Interaction.Behaviors> </TextBox> </Window>

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.FrameworkElementZoomFontSizeOnMouseWheelBehavior
member.summary: A behavior that enables zooming the font size of a using the mouse wheel while the Ctrl key is pressed.
member.remarks: This behavior adjusts the font size of the associated based on the mouse wheel delta. The font size is constrained within the range defined by and . To use this behavior, attach it to a and ensure the Ctrl key is held down while scrolling the mouse wheel.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.FrameworkElementZoomFontSizeOnMouseWheelBehavior.MaxFontSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum font size allowed for text rendering.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.FrameworkElementZoomFontSizeOnMouseWheelBehavior.MinFontSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum font size allowed for text rendering.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.FrameworkElementZoomFontSizeOnMouseWheelBehavior.OnAttached
member.summary: Called after the behavior is attached to an AssociatedObject.
member.remarks: Override this to hook up functionality to the AssociatedObject.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.FrameworkElementZoomFontSizeOnMouseWheelBehavior.OnDetaching
member.summary: Called when the behavior is being detached from its AssociatedObject, but before it has actually occurred.
member.remarks: Override this to unhook functionality from the AssociatedObject.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.FrameworkElementZoomFontSizeOnMouseWheelBehavior.AssociatedObject_PreviewMouseWheel(System.Object,System.Windows.Input.MouseWheelEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles the event to adjust the font size of the associated object when the Ctrl key is pressed and the mouse wheel is scrolled.
member.remarks: This method modifies the font size of the associated object based on the mouse wheel delta, provided the Ctrl key is held down. The font size is constrained within the range defined by and . If the font size reaches the minimum or maximum limit, no further adjustments are made.
member.param: The source of the event, typically the associated object.
The containing event data, including the mouse wheel delta.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.ItemsControlFilterBehavior
member.summary: Behavior that will allow a to filter a .
member.remarks:
Example XAML usage:
<Window xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation" xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml" xmlns:i="http://schemas.microsoft.com/xaml/behaviors" xmlns:behaviors="clr-namespace:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors;assembly=ApexGateUI">
<StackPanel> <TextBox x:Name="SearchBox" Width="200" Margin="0,0,0,10"> <i:Interaction.Behaviors> <behaviors:ItemsControlFilterBehavior TargetItemsControl="{Binding ElementName=MyListBox}" /> </i:Interaction.Behaviors> </TextBox>
<ListBox x:Name="MyListBox" ItemsSource="{Binding MyItems}" /> </StackPanel>
</Window>

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Behaviors.TextBoxCopyBehavior.OnButtonClick(System.Object,System.Windows.RoutedEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles the button click event to copy text from the target text box to the clipboard.
member.remarks: If the is null, the method exits without performing any action. The method prioritizes copying the selected text from the target text box. If no text is selected, it copies the entire text content of the text box. If the text to copy is empty or null, no action is taken. Any exceptions that occur while setting the clipboard text are logged using .
member.param: The source of the event, typically the button that was clicked.
The event data associated with the button click.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Collections.ConcurrentObservableCollection`1.System#Collections#IList#IsReadOnly
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the collection is read-only.
member.remarks: This property determines whether the collection can be modified or not. If the value is true, the collection is read-only and cannot be modified. If the value is false, the collection is not read-only and can be modified.
member.returns: true if the collection is read-only; otherwise, false.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Collections.ConcurrentObservableCollection`1.System#Collections#IList#IsFixedSize
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the collection has a fixed size.
member.remarks: The IsFixedSize property indicates whether the collection has a fixed size. A fixed-size collection does not allow elements to be added or removed once the collection is created. The value of this property is specific to the ConcurrentObservableCollection class.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Collections.ConcurrentObservableCollection`1.System#Collections#ICollection#SyncRoot
member.summary: Gets an object that can be used to synchronize access to the collection.
member.remarks: The SyncRoot property returns an object that can be used to synchronize access to the collection. This property is provided for compatibility with the property. In the case of the class, the property always returns the object itself, since the collection is already thread -safe and does not require additional synchronization.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Collections.ConcurrentObservableCollection`1.System#Collections#ICollection#IsSynchronized
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether access to the collection is synchronized (thread-safe).
member.remarks: This property returns true if the collection is thread-safe; otherwise, false. A thread-safe collection can be accessed by multiple threads concurrently, and it guarantees that no corruption will occur as a result of concurrent access. However, you still need to ensure proper synchronization when accessing the elements of the collection to avoid unpredictable results.

member.summary: Number close to zero, where float.MinValue is -float.MaxValue

member.summary: Determines whether the specified value is close to zero.
member.param: The value to evaluate.
member.returns: if the value is within the zero tolerance; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Common.DoubleUtil.AreClose(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns whether two doubles are "close". That is, whether they are within epsilon of each other. Note that this epsilon is proportional to the numbers themselves to that AreClose survives scalar multiplication.
member.param: The first double to compare.
The second double to compare.
member.returns: bool - the result of the AreClose comparision.
member.remarks: This is based on code from https://github.com/gix/PresentationTheme.Aero available via the MIT License

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Common.DoubleUtil.LessThan(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: LessThan - Returns whether the first double is less than the second double. That is, whether the first is strictly less than *and* not within epsilon of the other number. Note that this epsilon is proportional to the numbers themselves to that AreClose survives scalar multiplication.
member.param: The first value to compare.
The second value to compare.
member.returns: The result of the comparison.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Common.DoubleUtil.GreaterThan(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns whether the first double is greater than the second double. That is, whether the first is strictly greater than *and* not within epsilon of the other number. Note that this epsilon is proportional to the numbers themselves to that AreClose survives scalar multiplication.
member.param: The first value to compare.
The second value to compare.
member.returns: The result of the comparison.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Common.DoubleUtil.LessThanOrClose(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns whether the first double is less than or close to the second double. That is, whether the first is strictly less than or within epsilon of the other number. Note that this epsilon is proportional to the numbers themselves to that AreClose survives scalar multiplication.
member.param: The first value to compare.
The second value to compare.
member.returns: The result of the comparison.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Common.DoubleUtil.GreaterThanOrClose(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: GreaterThanOrClose - Returns whether the first double is greater than or close to the second double. That is, whether or not the first is strictly greater than or within epsilon of the other number. Note that this epsilon is proportional to the numbers themselves to that AreClose survives scalar multiplication.
member.param: The first value to compare.
The second value to compare.
member.returns: The result of the GreaterThanOrClose comparision.

member.summary: Determines whether the specified value is not a number.
member.param: The value to evaluate.
member.returns: if the value is not a number; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AdaptiveImage.RgbToHsl(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.summary: Converts RGB color values to their equivalent HSL (Hue, Saturation, Lightness) representation.
member.remarks: The RGB values must be normalized to the range [0, 1] before calling this method. The resulting HSL values are also normalized to the range [0, 1].
member.param: The red component of the color, in the range [0, 1].
The green component of the color, in the range [0, 1].
The blue component of the color, in the range [0, 1].
When this method returns, contains the hue of the color, in the range [0, 1].
When this method returns, contains the saturation of the color, in the range [0, 1].
When this method returns, contains the lightness of the color, in the range [0, 1].

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AdaptiveImage.HslToRgb(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.summary: Converts a color from HSL (Hue, Saturation, Lightness) color space to RGB (Red, Green, Blue) color space.
member.remarks: This method calculates the RGB representation of a color based on its HSL values. The resulting RGB values are normalized to the range [0.0, 1.0]. The hue value wraps around cyclically, so values outside the range [0.0, 1.0] are interpreted modulo 1.0.
member.param: The hue of the color, specified as a value between 0.0 and 1.0, where 0.0 represents 0 degrees and 1.0 represents 360 degrees.
The saturation of the color, specified as a value between 0.0 (completely unsaturated) and 1.0 (fully saturated).
The lightness of the color, specified as a value between 0.0 (completely dark) and 1.0 (completely light).
When the method returns, contains the red component of the color, as a value between 0.0 and 1.0.
When the method returns, contains the green component of the color, as a value between 0.0 and 1.0.
When the method returns, contains the blue component of the color, as a value between 0.0 and 1.0.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AdaptiveImage.Hue2Rgb(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Converts a hue value to its corresponding RGB component value.
member.remarks: This method is typically used as part of the HSL-to-RGB color conversion process. The input parameter represents the adjusted hue for a specific RGB channel, and the method calculates the corresponding intensity for that channel.
member.param: The first intermediate value used in the RGB conversion process. Typically derived from the lightness and saturation of the color.
The second intermediate value used in the RGB conversion process. Typically derived from the lightness and saturation of the color.
The hue value to be converted, represented as a fractional value in the range [0, 1].
member.returns: The calculated RGB component value as a double in the range [0, 1].

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayer
member.summary: An audio player control with a familiar transport layout: a centered Previous / Play-Stop / Next button row sitting above a full-width seek slider that is flanked by the current playback time and the total track length.
member.remarks: Playback is backed by which supports the common audio formats handled by Windows Media Foundation (.mp3, .wav, .wma, .aac, etc.). The control manages an internal so that the Previous / Next buttons (and the / helper methods) navigate between tracks. Programmatic control is available via the , , , , and methods.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayer.Seek(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Seeks the active track to the specified , updating the reported , the seek slider and the underlying audio position so that they all reflect the requested point. The seek is honored whether or not the player is currently playing. If playback is in progress it is briefly paused, repositioned and then resumed so the new position takes effect cleanly; otherwise the underlying audio is repositioned in place so a subsequent resumes from the requested point.
member.param: The position to seek to. Values are clamped to the track's duration.
member.remarks: If a has been assigned but the media has not finished opening yet (so its is not known), the seek is remembered and applied automatically once the media opens. This makes it safe to set and immediately seek to a timestamp.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayer.UpdatePosition(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Updates the reported and the seek slider without triggering a seek back into the player.

member.summary: Exposes to UI Automation, surfacing an that toggles playback between playing and stopped.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerAutomationPeer.Invoke
member.summary: Toggles playback: stops if currently playing, otherwise starts playback.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer
member.summary: A real-time spectrum analyzer designed to pair with the control. It renders frequency bands across the horizontal axis and signal strength (amplitude) on the vertical axis, with optional peak-hold indicators and amplitude-driven color intensity that makes strong frequencies easy to identify at a glance.
member.remarks: The analyzer captures the audio rendered by the default Windows output device using WASAPI loopback — the same capture infrastructure used by . Because the plays through that device, simply placing this control near an visualizes whatever it is currently playing (along with any other system audio). A short-time Fourier transform converts the captured samples into a magnitude spectrum which is then grouped into logarithmically spaced bands so that the bass, mid, and treble ranges are all legible.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer.FftSize
member.summary: The number of samples fed into each FFT. Must be a power of two.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer.DecibelFloor
member.summary: The lowest signal level (in decibels relative to full scale) mapped to the bottom of a bar.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer.FrameIntervalMilliseconds
member.summary: The interval between visual updates, expressed in milliseconds (~30 fps).

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer.AttackRate
member.summary: How quickly a bar rises toward a higher target value (0 = frozen, 1 = instant).

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer.DecayRate
member.summary: How quickly a bar falls toward a lower target value (0 = frozen, 1 = instant).

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer.PeakFallPerFrame
member.summary: How far a peak-hold marker drops each frame once it is no longer being pushed up.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer._bandValues
member.summary: Per-band smoothed magnitudes in the range 0..1, used to draw the bars.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer._peakValues
member.summary: Per-band peak-hold markers in the range 0..1.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer._bandBinStart
member.summary: The first FFT bin (inclusive) contributing to each band.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer._bandBinEnd
member.summary: The last FFT bin (exclusive) contributing to each band.

member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer.IsActive

member.summary: Gets or sets the vertical gradient used to color bars when is enabled. The gradient is mapped to the full height of the control so each bar reveals the portion of the gradient up to its amplitude — the default is the classic green (quiet) through yellow and orange to red (loudest).

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer.UpdateBandValues
member.summary: Pulls the most recent captured samples, runs the FFT, and folds the magnitude spectrum into the smoothed per-band values and peak-hold markers that drive the next frame.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer.ComputeBandTargets
member.summary: Computes the normalized 0..1 magnitude for each band from the latest captured audio, or an empty span when the analyzer is idle so that the bars decay naturally to zero.

member.summary: Produces a static, pleasant-looking set of band magnitudes so the control previews well in designers.

member.summary: Performs an in-place iterative radix-2 Cooley-Tukey FFT on the supplied complex arrays.
member.param: The real components; overwritten with the transform's real output.
The imaginary components; overwritten with the transform's imaginary output.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.AudioPlayerSpectrumAnalyzer.CreateIntensityGradient
member.summary: Builds the default bottom-to-top intensity gradient: green for quiet levels, rising through yellow and orange to red at the loudest, echoing the look of classic spectrum analyzers.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.TypingProgress
member.summary: Represents a control that visually indicates typing progress, typically used in chat or messaging scenarios.
member.remarks:
The control displays an animated visual effect to indicate that typing is in progress. The animation is controlled by the property, which determines whether the animation is active. The control uses a storyboard resource named "TypingStoryboard" to define the animation, which must be present in the control's resources. The control automatically adjusts its visibility based on the value of :
When is , the control becomes visible and the animation starts.
When is , the control becomes hidden and the animation stops.

member.summary: Stores the previous clip object for internal use.
member.remarks: This field is used to retain a reference to the old clip, typically for comparison or restoration purposes. It is intended for internal use and should not be accessed directly outside of the containing class.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.ClipBorder.OnApplyChildClip
member.summary: Applies a clipping geometry to the child element based on the border thickness and corner radius.
member.remarks: This method calculates a rounded rectangle geometry using the current border thickness and corner radius, and applies it as a clipping region to the child element. If no child element is present, no action is taken.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.ClipBorder.GetRoundRectangle(System.Windows.Rect,System.Windows.Thickness,System.Windows.CornerRadius)
member.summary: Creates a representing a rectangle with rounded corners, taking into account the specified border thickness and corner radii.
member.remarks: The method normalizes the corner radii and border thickness to ensure they are non-negative. If the corner radii or border thickness values are too small, they are treated as zero. The resulting geometry is frozen for performance optimization and can be used directly in rendering operations.
member.param: The base rectangle that defines the overall dimensions of the rounded rectangle.
The thickness of the border, which affects the size of the rounded corners.
The radii of the corners, specifying how rounded each corner should be.
member.returns: A object representing the rounded rectangle. The geometry includes arcs for the rounded corners and straight lines for the edges between the corners.

member.returns: A frozen , or null if an icon could not be resolved.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.Files
member.summary: A lookless control that lists the contents of a directory using a three-column view: Name (with the operating-system shell icon for the file type), Date Modified, and Size (formatted in friendly units such as 525 KB or 2.1 MB). When folder navigation is enabled (see , on by default) sub-folders are listed with a folder icon and a .. parent entry, and double-clicking a folder navigates into it one level at a time; this behavior can be toggled off to list files only. It supports single or multiple selection, an optional file-system watcher that keeps the listing in sync with the folder, a manual method, and raises (with the ) when a file is double-clicked.

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether a monitors the directory and automatically refreshes the listing when its contents change. Disabled by default.

member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.Files.ShowFolders
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether folder navigation is enabled. When true (the default), sub-folders are listed alongside files — each shown with a folder icon — and a .. entry is added when a parent folder exists. Double-clicking (or pressing on) a folder navigates into it, replacing the listing with that folder's contents and moving keyboard focus to the list; the .. entry navigates up to the parent. Only one folder's contents are shown at a time (this is a flat listing, not a tree view). Set this to false to list files only and disable folder navigation entirely.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.Files.RootDirectoryProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.Files.RootDirectory
member.summary: Gets or sets the full path of a folder that bounds upward navigation. When set, the user can browse this folder and its sub-folders (and back up toward it) but cannot navigate above it: the .. parent entry is hidden once the listing reaches this folder, and any attempt to navigate to a folder outside this subtree is ignored. Leave empty (the default) to allow navigation anywhere the file system permits. Has no effect when is false.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.Files.ShowNavigateErrorMessageBoxProperty

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.Files.ShowNavigateErrorMessageBox
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether a warning showing the error message is displayed to the user when folder navigation fails. This is independent of the event, which is always raised regardless of this setting. The default is true.

member.summary: Occurs when folder navigation fails (for example the target folder no longer exists or access is denied). The event arguments carry the attempted path and the . The failure is handled gracefully so it never propagates to the host application.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.Files.ApplyItemContainerStyle
member.summary: Builds the item-container style used to attach the per-row context menu. The style derives (via ) from the themed style currently in scope so Mosaic's theming is preserved, then layers on the context-menu selection: files use , folders use , and the .. parent-navigation entry shows none. The themed style is resolved here at runtime — rather than with a XAML StaticResource in the template — because the native control styles are merged into the resource scope dynamically and are not present when this control's template is parsed.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.Files.BuildContextMenuTrigger(System.String,System.Object,System.Windows.DependencyProperty)
member.summary: Creates a that, when the bound property equals , sets each row's to the menu held by on this control (bound so later changes propagate).

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.Files.NavigateToFolder(System.String)
member.summary: Navigates into the supplied folder, replacing the listing with that folder's contents and moving keyboard focus into the list. The folder is probed for access up front so that any failure is surfaced here — raising (and optionally a warning message box) without changing the current directory — instead of crashing the host application.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.GridUtils
member.summary: Provides utility methods and attached properties for dynamically defining row and column definitions in a control.
member.remarks:
Example usage in XAML: <Grid xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation" xmlns:utils="clr-namespace:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls;assembly=Mosaic.UI.Wpf" utils:GridUtils.RowDefinitions="Auto,*,2*" utils:GridUtils.ColumnDefinitions="100,*,Auto"> <!-- Child elements placed using Grid.Row and Grid.Column --> <TextBlock Grid.Row="0" Grid.Column="0" Text="Header" /> <ListBox Grid.Row="1" Grid.Column="1" /> </Grid>
Notes: - Row/column sizes are comma-separated. Empty entry yields default (Auto). - Use '*' suffix for star sizing (e.g. "2*" or "*" for 1*). - Use "Auto" for Auto sizing or a numeric pixel value (e.g. "100") for fixed pixels.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.OnHexColorChanged(System.Windows.DependencyObject,System.Windows.DependencyPropertyChangedEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles changes to the dependency property.
member.remarks: This method normalizes the new hex color value, updates the property, and synchronizes the text displayed in the editable text box, if applicable. If the new value is invalid, no changes are applied. The method prevents recursive updates by using an internal flag.
member.param: The object on which the property value has changed. This is expected to be an instance of .
Provides data about the property change, including the old and new values.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.OnColorChanged(System.Windows.DependencyObject,System.Windows.DependencyPropertyChangedEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles changes to the control's color value when the associated dependency property changes.
member.remarks: This method updates the control's internal state and UI to reflect the new color value. It synchronizes the hex color representation, updates the color shades, and ensures the editable text box and selected item are consistent with the new color.
member.param: The on which the property change occurred. This should be an instance of .
The event data containing information about the property change, including the old and new values.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.OnApplyTemplate
member.summary: ApplyTemplate wiring: finds the editable TextBox inside the ComboBox template and wires up events. Also ensures color shades reflect the current Color.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.OnSelectionChanged(System.Windows.Controls.SelectionChangedEventArgs)
member.summary: Called when the selection changes in the combo box. Updates the control's Color and HexColor properties to match the newly selected color item.
member.param: Selection changed event data.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.OnEditableTextChanged(System.Object,System.Windows.Controls.TextChangedEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles the event for the editable text box, updating the color value and normalizing the hex color string.
member.remarks: This method validates and parses the text input as a hexadecimal color value. If the input is valid, it updates the and properties and ensures the text box displays the normalized hex color string. The method prevents recursive updates by using an internal flag.
member.param: The source of the event, typically the editable text box.
The event data containing information about the text change.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.OnEditableLostFocus(System.Object,System.Windows.RoutedEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles the loss of focus event for the editable text box, validating and normalizing the entered hex color value.
member.remarks: If the entered text represents a valid hex color, it is normalized and applied to the HexColor and Color properties. If the text is invalid, the text box reverts to the previous valid value.
member.param: The source of the event, typically the editable text box.
The event data associated with the .

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.OnEditablePreviewKeyDown(System.Object,System.Windows.Input.KeyEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles the event for the editable text box.
member.remarks:
This method processes specific key inputs when the editable text box is focused:
If the key is pressed, the text box reverts to its previous value, and the associated color properties are restored.
If the key is pressed, the focus is moved to the next focusable element, simulating a "commit" action. The event is marked as handled () for these specific keys to prevent further processing.
member.param: The source of the event, typically the editable text box.
The instance containing the event data.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.UpdateColorShades(System.Windows.Media.Color)
member.summary: Updates the collection of color shades based on the specified base color.
member.remarks: This method generates 10 lighter shades, the base color, and 10 darker shades, and updates the collection and the collection accordingly. The lighter shades are added in descending order of lightness, followed by the base color, and then the darker shades in ascending order of darkness.
member.param: The base color used to generate lighter and darker shades.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.LightenColor(System.Windows.Media.Color,System.Double)
member.summary: Adjusts the brightness of the specified color by increasing its RGB components proportionally.
member.param: The to be lightened.
A value between 0 and 1 that determines the degree of lightening. A value of 0 returns the original color, while a value of 1 results in white.
member.returns: A new instance that is a lighter version of the input color.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.DarkenColor(System.Windows.Media.Color,System.Double)
member.summary: Darkens the specified color by reducing its RGB components based on the given factor.
member.param: The to be darkened.
A value between 0 and 1 representing the percentage by which to darken the color. A factor of 0 leaves the color unchanged, while a factor of 1 results in black.
member.returns: A new instance that is a darkened version of the input color.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.TryParseHexColor(System.String,System.Windows.Media.Color@,System.String@)
member.summary: Attempts to parse a string representation of a color into a structure.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HexColorTextBox.ColorToHex(System.Windows.Media.Color)
member.summary: Converts a object to its hexadecimal string representation, including the alpha channel.
member.param: The to convert to a hexadecimal string.
member.returns: A string representing the color in hexadecimal format, including the alpha channel, in the form "#AARRGGBB".

member.summary: Provides a value converter that extracts display text from a object for use in data binding scenarios.
member.remarks: This converter is typically used to display the text or URI of a in UI elements. If the property is not set, the converter will fall back to the property. If neither is available, an empty string is returned.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HyperlinkTextConverter.Instance
member.summary: Static instance of the for use in XAML.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HyperlinkToolTipConverter
member.summary: Provides a mechanism to convert a object into a tooltip string that describes its behavior or destination. This converter is commonly used in XAML to dynamically generate tooltips for hyperlinks based on their properties.
member.remarks:
The conversion logic determines the tooltip content based on the properties of the :
If the property is set, its value is returned.
If the property is set, its string representation is returned to indicate the destination URI.
If the property is set, a default message is returned to inform the user that the hyperlink will execute application-defined code. If none of these properties are set, the converter returns .

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.HyperlinkToolTipConverter.Instance
member.summary: Static instance of the for use in XAML.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.InertiaScrollViewer
member.summary: Represents a scroll viewer that supports inertia-based scrolling animations.
member.remarks:
The extends the class to provide smooth, animated scrolling behavior when the property is enabled. This is particularly useful for scenarios where a more fluid scrolling experience is desired, such as in touch-based or mouse-wheel interactions.
This is based on code from https://github.com/WPFDevelopersOrg/WPFDevelopers available via the MIT License.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.InertiaScrollViewer.AnimateScroll(System.Double,System.Action)
member.summary: Animates the vertical scroll position to a specified value over a fixed duration.
member.remarks: The animation uses a cubic easing function with an "EaseOut" mode to create a smooth deceleration effect. The animation duration is fixed at 800 milliseconds, and the frame rate is limited to 40 frames per second.
member.param: The target vertical offset to scroll to.
An optional callback that is invoked when the animation completes. If not provided, no action is taken upon completion.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.MarkdownViewer
member.summary: A lookless, WPF-native Markdown viewer. Markdown supplied via the property is rendered into a and hosted in a read-only , so the formatted content can be selected and copied as rich text instead of raw Markdown syntax. Rendering is defensive: invalid Markdown never throws, and a failed render falls back to displaying the original text as plain text.

member.summary: Called when the owner object's property changes (Owner → UI direction). This method updates the PropertyItem's Value to reflect changes made directly to the owner object. Triggered when: The owner object's property is modified programmatically outside the PropertyGrid.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.PropertyItem.SetOwnerPropertyValue(System.Object)
member.summary: Sets the value of the property identified by on the owner object.
member.remarks: This method attempts to locate a writable property on the owner object with the name specified by . If the property is found and writable, the provided value is converted to the property's type and assigned to it. If the property does not exist, is not writable, or an error occurs during the operation, the method fails silently.
member.param: The value to set for the property. The value will be converted to the property's type if necessary.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.PropertyItem.TrySetOwnerPropertyValue(System.Object)
member.summary: Attempts to set the value of the property on the owner object.
member.param: The value to set.
member.returns: True if the value was set successfully; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.PropertyItem.ConvertValue(System.Object,System.Type)
member.summary: Converts the specified value to the specified target type, handling nullable types and using type converters where applicable.
member.remarks: This method first checks if the value is already of the target type and returns it directly if so. For nullable types, it handles empty string values by returning . If a type converter is available for the target type, it is used for the conversion. Otherwise, the method falls back to for the conversion.
member.param: The value to convert. Can be .
The type to which the value should be converted. Must not be .
member.returns: The converted value as an instance of , or if is or an empty string and the target type is nullable.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.RelativePanel
member.summary: Defines an area within which you can position and align child objects in relation to each other or the parent panel.

remarks.para: By default, any unconstrained element declared as a child of the RelativePanel is given the entire available space and positioned at the(0, 0) coordinates(upper left corner) of the panel.So, if you are positioning a second element relative to an unconstrained element, keep in mind that the second element might get pushed out of the panel.

If you set multiple relationships that target the same edge of an element, you might have conflicting relationships in your layout as a result.When this happens, the relationships are applied in the following order of priority: • Panel alignment relationships (AlignTopWithPanel, AlignLeftWithPanel, …) are applied first. • Sibling alignment relationships(AlignTopWith, AlignLeftWith, …) are applied second. • Sibling positional relationships(Above, Below, RightOf, LeftOf) are applied last.
The panel-center alignment properties(AlignVerticalCenterWith, AlignHorizontalCenterWithPanel, ...) are typically used independently of other constraints and are applied if there is no conflict.

The HorizontalAlignment and VerticalAlignment properties on UI elements are applied after relationship properties are evaluated and applied. These properties control the placement of the element within the available size for the element, if the desired size is smaller than the available size.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.RelativePanel.GetAbove(System.Windows.DependencyObject)
member.summary: Gets the value of the RelativePanel.Above XAML attached property for the target element.
member.param: The object from which the property value is read.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.RelativePanel.MeasureOverride(System.Windows.Size)
member.summary: When overridden in a derived class, measures the size in layout required for child elements and determines a size for the System.Windows.FrameworkElement-derived class.
member.param: The available size that this element can give to child elements. Infinity can be specified as a value to indicate that the element will size to whatever content is available.
member.returns: The size that this element determines it needs during layout, based on its calculations of child element sizes.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.RelativePanel.ArrangeOverride(System.Windows.Size)
member.summary: When overridden in a derived class, positions child elements and determines a size for a System.Windows.FrameworkElement derived class.
member.param: The final area within the parent that this element should use to arrange itself and its children.
member.returns: The actual size used.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.RelativePanelGraph.CalculateDesiredSize
member.summary: Starting at zero, we will traverse all the horizontal and vertical chains in the graph one by one while moving a "cursor" up and down as we go in jumps that are equivalent to the size of the element associated to the node that we are currently visiting. Everytime that the cursor hits a new max or new min value, we cache it. At the end, the difference between the max and the min values corresponds to the desired size of that chain. The size of the biggest chain also corresponds to the desired size of the panel.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.ScalingTextBlock
member.summary: A that will attempt to scale the font size so that all text fits. and are used as upper and lower boundaries.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.ScalingTextBlock.MinFontSize
member.summary: The smallest font size that should be used. The default is 12.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.ScalingTextBlock.MaxFontSize
member.summary: The largest font size that should be used. The default is 24.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.ScalingTextBlock.FontScaleIncrement
member.summary: The value that should be used to increment scaling (e.g. 1 would attempt to lower by 1 font size when measuring text against the available width). Lower numbers require more checks but choose a more precise size. The default is 1.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.NestedItemMarginConverter
member.summary: Converts a to a value representing its left margin, based on its nesting level within parent containers named "ChildrenContainer".
member.remarks: This converter calculates the left margin for a nested item in a hierarchical structure. The margin is determined by the item's nesting level within parent containers named "ChildrenContainer". The base margin is 3 pixels, with an additional 20 pixels added for each level of nesting.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.SmallPanel
member.summary:
Represents a custom panel that arranges its child elements in a single layer and ensures that each child is measured and arranged within the available space.
Use cases: - Overlay multiple visuals on top of each other (for example decorations, adorners, or layered effects) where all children should share the same bounds rather than be stacked or tiled. - When you want a simple single-layer container that sizes to the largest child instead of stacking children vertically or horizontally as StackPanel does. - Useful for templated controls, backgrounds, or overlays where deterministic measure/arrange behavior and consistent sizing are required without the complexity of a Grid. - A lightweight alternative when only one layout layer is needed and you want child elements to occupy the same available area.
member.remarks: This is based on code from https://github.com/WPFDevelopersOrg/WPFDevelopers available via the MIT License.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.SplitPanel
member.summary: A two-pane container, similar in usage to a uniform panel, whose panes are separated by a draggable . The proportion of space allocated to the first pane is exposed through the two-way dependency property (0.0 = first pane collapsed, 1.0 = second pane collapsed). Set to (the default) to stack the panes top/bottom, or to place them side-by-side.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.SplitPanel._isDragging
member.summary: True while the user is actively dragging the splitter. While dragging the splitter owns the grid lengths directly, so changes are observed rather than applied to avoid fighting the live drag.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.SplitPanel.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.summary: Rebuilds the grid's row/column definitions for the current and applies the current , and .

member.summary: Pushes the current onto the two star-sized definitions.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.SplitPanel.UpdatePositionFromDefinitions
member.summary: Reads the realized sizes of the two panes after a drag and reflects the ratio back into without re-applying it to the (already correct) grid lengths.

member.summary: Gets or sets the content of the first pane (top when is vertical, left when horizontal).
member.value: The content of the first pane.

member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.SplitPanel.SplitterPosition
member.summary: Gets or sets the proportion of the available space allocated to , expressed as a value between 0.0 and 1.0. A value of 0.0 collapses the first pane (0% / 100%), 1.0 collapses the second pane (100% / 0%) and 0.5 splits the space evenly. The value updates live as the user drags the splitter. This property is two-way by default.
member.value: The fraction (0.0 - 1.0) of the panel allocated to the first pane.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.SyntaxEditor
member.summary: A code editor built on AvalonEdit's that integrates with the Mosaic theming system and provides bundled, theme-aware syntax highlighting selected via the property.
member.remarks:
Custom HotKeys/Chords:
- Comment Selection: Ctrl+K, Ctrl+C - Uncomment Selection: Ctrl+K, Ctrl+U - Move Line or Selection Up: Ctrl+Up - Move Line or Selection Down: Ctrl+Down
The editor defaults to Consolas 12 with line numbers enabled. Colors for the editing surface (background, foreground, line numbers, selection, current line) are derived from the Mosaic theme indicated by . When is true (the default) the editor also tracks .

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.TagBox
member.summary: A specialized input control that turns typed text into removable, vibrantly-colored "tags". Pressing commits the current text as a new tag, each tag carries an "✕" button to remove it, and pressing while the caret sits just to the right of the tags removes the last one. Tags are surfaced through the bindable collection, and the / events allow callers to veto or observe every change.

member.summary: The text entry portion of the control where new tags are typed.

member.summary: The panel that hosts the tag chips followed by the input .

member.summary: Raised before a tag is added to or removed from the collection. Set to to veto the change.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.ViewMode
member.summary: Specifies the available layout modes for displaying content panes within the user interface.
member.remarks: Use this enumeration to control how panes are arranged or shown in the UI. Each value represents a distinct arrangement, such as displaying only one pane or splitting the view horizontally or vertically. This enumeration is intended for internal use within the application.

member.summary: Specifies a horizontal orientation with left and right directions.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.ViewMode.RightLeft
member.summary: Specifies that content flows from right to left.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.ViewMode.TopBottom
member.summary: Specifies that the layout or operation applies to both the top and bottom edges or positions.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.ViewMode.BottomTop
member.summary: Specifies that layout or content flows from the bottom to the top.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.ViewMode.None

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.WaveformVisualizer.InputWaveformVisualizer
member.summary: Displays a waveform captured from a selectable Windows audio input device.
member.remarks: The visualizer uses WASAPI shared-mode capture so other applications can use the selected input device concurrently.

example.code: <ComboBox ItemsSource="{Binding InputDevices, ElementName=Visualizer}" SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedInputDevice, ElementName=Visualizer}" /> <mosaic:InputWaveformVisualizer x:Name="Visualizer" />

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.WindowTitleBar
member.summary: A self-contained custom title bar control for borderless/chrome-less WPF windows. Drop it into the first row of your window layout and it automatically wires up drag-to-move, double-click maximize/restore, and the standard window system buttons.
member.remarks:
The control locates its parent on and detaches cleanly on , so no code-behind is required in the host window.
If is not set the control automatically mirrors through a live binding.

example.code: <!-- Row 0 of your window grid, spanning all columns --> <mosaic:WindowTitleBar Grid.Row="0" Grid.ColumnSpan="2" IconSource="/Assets/app-48.png" TitleText="My Application"> <mosaic:WindowTitleBar.RightContent> <!-- Extra buttons that appear to the left of the system buttons --> <Button Click="OnSettingsClick" Content="⚙" /> </mosaic:WindowTitleBar.RightContent> </mosaic:WindowTitleBar>

member.summary: WindowTitleBar

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Controls.WindowTitleBar.TitleTextProperty
member.summary: Gets or sets the title text displayed in the title bar. When or empty the control automatically mirrors the parent .

member.summary: Gets or sets the horizontal alignment of the title text.

member.summary: Gets or sets the image source used for the title bar icon.

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the title bar icon is visible.

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the minimize button is visible.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Converters.BoolToBrushConverter
member.summary: Converts a boolean value to a brush specified by hex values. The ConverterParameter can be used to specify the hex value brushes, delimited by comma, semicolon, or pipe.
member.remarks:
Example usage in XAML:
<Window.Resources> <local:BoolToBrushConverter x:Key="BoolToBrushConverter" /> </Window.Resources>
<TextBlock Text="{Binding IsEnabled, Converter={StaticResource BoolToBrushConverter}, ConverterParameter='#267F00,#FF0000'}" />

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Converters.BoolToLabelConverter
member.summary: Converts a boolean value to a specified label for true/false. The ConverterParameter can be used to specify the labels, delimited by comma, semicolon, or pipe.
member.remarks:
Example usage in XAML:
<Window.Resources> <local:BoolToLabelConverter x:Key="BoolToLabelConverter" /> </Window.Resources>
<TextBlock Text="{Binding IsEnabled, Converter={StaticResource BoolToLabelConverter}, ConverterParameter='Enabled,Disabled'}" />

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Extensions.ColorExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for converting objects to string representations.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Extensions.ColorExtensions.ToHexString(System.Windows.Media.Color,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Converts the specified to a hexadecimal color string.
member.param: The to convert.
If true, the returned string includes the alpha channel as the first two hex digits (format: #AARRGGBB). If false, the returned string contains only the RGB channels (format: #RRGGBB).
member.returns: A string containing the hexadecimal representation of the color prefixed with '#'. Examples: #FF3366 (RGB only) or #80FF3366 (including alpha). Hex values are uppercase and each channel is zero-padded to two characters.
member.remarks: This extension targets WPF (System.Windows.Media.Color). Uses uppercase hexadecimal digits and ensures two characters per channel.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Extensions.ColorExtensions.ToRgbString(System.Windows.Media.Color)
member.summary: Converts the specified to a CSS-style RGB string.
member.param: The to convert. This is the WPF System.Windows.Media.Color.
member.returns: A string in the form rgb(R, G, B) where R, G, and B are the red, green, and blue channel values from (0–255). The alpha channel is ignored; use an RGBA or hex representation if alpha is required.
member.remarks: Produces a CSS-compatible RGB representation suitable for use in web/CSS contexts or any textual representation that expects integers for each color channel. Each channel is formatted as a decimal integer with no leading zeros or padding.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Extensions.ColorExtensions.ToHexString(System.Drawing.Color,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Converts the specified to a hexadecimal color string.
member.param: The to convert.
If true, the returned string includes the alpha channel as the first two hex digits (format: #AARRGGBB). If false, the returned string contains only the RGB channels (format: #RRGGBB).
member.returns: A string containing the hexadecimal representation of the color prefixed with '#'. Examples: #FF3366 (RGB only) or #80FF3366 (including alpha). Hex values are uppercase and each channel is zero-padded to two characters.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Extensions.ColorExtensions.ToRgbString(System.Drawing.Color)
member.summary: Converts the specified to a CSS-style RGB string.
member.param: The to convert.
member.returns: A string in the form rgb(R, G, B) where R, G, and B are the red, green, and blue channel values from (0–255). The alpha channel is ignored; use an RGBA or hex representation if alpha is required.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Themes.DocumentViewerNativeHelper
member.summary: Attached behavior that applies the Mosaic native style to a 's built-in find toolbar (the bar shown for Ctrl+F).
member.remarks: WPF's find toolbar (MS.Internal.Documents.FindToolBar) is loaded from PresentationUI and declares its own {x:Type ToolBar} style in its element resources. That local style shadows the Mosaic native ToolBar style, so the find toolbar's chrome (background, grip, overflow) is not themed by default. There is no XAML/theme hook to override element-local resources on a framework-internal control, so this helper reaches the hosted toolbar through the public template part and assigns the themed style explicitly. It is wired automatically by the native DocumentViewer style; it is a no-op when the themed style cannot be found.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Themes.DocumentViewerNativeHelper.ThemeFindToolBarProperty
member.summary: Identifies the ThemeFindToolBar attached dependency property.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Themes.DocumentViewerNativeHelper.SetThemeFindToolBar(System.Windows.DependencyObject,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets a value indicating whether the DocumentViewer's find toolbar should be styled with the Mosaic native ToolBar style.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Themes.DocumentViewerNativeHelper.GetThemeFindToolBar(System.Windows.DependencyObject)
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the DocumentViewer's find toolbar should be styled with the Mosaic native ToolBar style.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Themes.DocumentViewerNativeHelper.ApplyFindToolBarStyle(System.Windows.Controls.DocumentViewer)
member.summary: Locates the hosted find toolbar and applies the themed ToolBar style to it.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.Themes.ThemeManager
member.summary: Manages Mosaic theme resources and supports runtime theme switching.
member.remarks: This dictionary is self-contained and updates its own collection only. This lets consumers scope theme resources at the application, window, or control level without mutating unrelated resource dictionaries.
